evidence.snippet: MicroLink Devices designs and manufactures solar cells and solar sheets for a variety of applications, including unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), spacecraft, and terrestrial collectors.

MicroLink Devices, a leader in the production of high-efficiency, flexible solar arrays for satellite and aircraft, was recognized by Airbus Defence and Space as a key supplier for the Zephyr High Altitude Pseudo-Satellite (HAPS) UAV platform. MicroLink and Airbus celebrated this event with employees on October 1, 2019, in a ceremony near MicroLink’s Chicago headquarters.

items.notes: High-efficiency flexible solar cells and solar sheets (ELO IMM technology) enabling solar-powered UAV flight. Supplied to Airbus Zephyr HAPS and Prismatic PHASA-35 HALE UAVs.

products_categories.notes: Primary product is ELO-based solar cells/sheets for UAV power. RF Semiconductor Materials (HBTs) are a secondary line targeting the telecom industry, not drone-specific.

MicroLink Devices designs and manufactures solar cells and solar sheets for a variety of applications, including unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), spacecraft, and terrestrial collectors.

MicroLink Devices, a leader in the production of high-efficiency, flexible solar arrays for satellite and aircraft, was recognized by Airbus Defence and Space as a key supplier for the Zephyr High Altitude Pseudo-Satellite (HAPS) UAV platform. MicroLink and Airbus celebrated this event with employees on October 1, 2019, in a ceremony near MicroLink’s Chicago headquarters.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Supplies solar cell/sheet components to UAV OEMs (Airbus for Zephyr HAPS UAV, Prismatic for PHASA-35 HALE UAV). Does not manufacture complete UAVs.

items.name: Photovoltaics (ELO IMM Solar Cells and Solar Sheets)
items.product_type: high-efficiency flexible solar cells and solar sheets for UAVs and spacecraft

evidence.snippet: MicroLink Devices designs and manufactures solar cells and solar sheets for a variety of applications, including unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), spacecraft, and terrestrial collectors.

items.notes: IMM = inverted metamorphic multi-junction; ELO = epitaxial lift-off. Specific power >2000 W/kg. Used on Airbus Zephyr and Prismatic PHASA-35.

items.name: RF Semiconductor Materials
items.product_type: compound semiconductor materials (HBTs, BiFETs, power amplifiers)
items.notes: Primarily for high-speed communications industry; not drone-specific per corpus.

items.name: Engineering Services
items.product_type: custom semiconductor device design and simulation services
items.notes: Transistor structure and solar cell design services for customers.

products.notes: Product pages (photovoltaics-2, rf-semiconductor-materials, engineering-services) were skipped; additional SKU detail likely on those pages.

ndaa.notes: Certifications page only mentions ISO 9001 since 2005. No NDAA Section 848/889 claim anywhere in the corpus.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS or Blue sUAS mention found in the corpus.

evidence.snippet: These high-efficiency, single- and multi-junction GaAs-based solar cells are manufactured using MicroLink’s proprietary epitaxial lift-off (ELO) technology, in which the solar cell structure is removed from the substrate on which it is grown.

readiness.notes: Site states solar cells 'are manufactured' using proprietary ELO technology; news confirms completed production contract delivery to Prismatic for PHASA-35 HALE UAV.

unresolved_questions: Whether any product lines are explicitly NDAA 848/889 compliant (certifications page only mentions ISO 9001)
Full SKU details for ELO IMM solar cell product variants (photovoltaics-2 page skipped)
Whether RF semiconductor materials have any drone/UAS-specific product lines

fetch_requests.reason: Would provide specific product SKUs, efficiency ratings, and specifications for ELO IMM solar cells to improve product list completeness and confirm UAV-specific product lines.
Would clarify whether RF semiconductor materials include drone-specific products or are exclusively for telecom, affecting category and supply chain role assessments.
Would clarify scope and named deliverables of engineering services offering, and whether NDAA compliance is mentioned.
About page would confirm headquarters details and may include compliance or Blue UAS disclosures.

tagline: Manufactures high-efficiency GaAs-based photovoltaic cells and sheets for UAVs, spacecraft, and terrestrial use via proprietary epitaxial lift-off technology; also supplies RF semiconductor materials and engineering services. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown. Limited public evidence on defense contracts or program adoption.
